title: Maintain the counter of a for loop in its increment clause
description: The counter of a for loop should be updated in the loop's increment clause. If the counter is not updated, the loop will be infinite with a constant counter variable. If this is intentional, use a while or do while loop instead of a for loop. If the counter variable is updated within the loop's body, try to move it to the increment clause. If this is impossible due to certain conditions, replace the for loop with a while or do while loop.
recommendation: Update the counter in the for loop's increment clause or replace the for loop with a while or do while loop.
impact: Infinite loops can cause unintended behavior and consume resources.

checks.message: Update the counter in the for loop's increment clause or replace the for loop with a while or do while loop.
